perturbation.original_text: The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns, trade discounts and cash discounts; but no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'trade discounts and cash discounts; but no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts' from the definition of “net wholesale volume,” the calculation becomes ambiguous. Section 3(a) defines how royalty is calculated on the net wholesale volume and how sales discounts should be considered, but because the definition of ‘net wholesale volume’ is changed it becomes unclear on how royalty payments are calculated, therefore making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "net wholesale volume" as gross sales less returns, trade discounts, and cash discounts, with no deduction for other discounts or uncollectible accounts. The changed text narrows this definition to only gross sales less returns. The contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text) uses this new, narrower definition. Although they are identical, it causes a contradiction because the explanation states that section 3(a) defines how royalty is calculated on the net wholesale volume and how sales discounts should be considered, but because the definition of ‘net wholesale volume’ is changed it becomes unclear on how royalty payments are calculated, therefore making enforcement uncertain. This fits the Omissions - In Text Contradiction perturbation type because essential language related to deductions is removed.

perturbation.original_text: Travel expenses of KI in connection with all scheduled personal appearances under this Agreement, as well the travel expenses of KI's child, the child's nanny, KI's hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing, and two (2) additional traveling companions of KI's choosing (collectively referred to as KI's "entourage"), shall be provided by Diplomat. KI and the members of her entourage shall travel via first class air and portal-to-portal limousine ground transportation. In addition, KI and the members of her entourage shall be lodged in first class hotel accommodations and all of them shall be reimbursed for all meals and other incidental expenses in connection with such appearances. All the above travel expenses of KI and the members of her entourage shall be billed directly to Diplomat.
perturbation.changed_text: Travel expenses of KI in connection with all scheduled personal appearances under this Agreement, as well the travel expenses of KI's child shall be provided by Diplomat. KI and the members of her entourage shall travel via first class air and portal-to-portal limousine ground transportation. In addition, KI and the members of her entourage shall be lodged in first class hotel accommodations and all of them shall be reimbursed for all meals and other incidental expenses in connection with such appearances. All the above travel expenses of KI and the members of her entourage shall be billed directly to Diplomat.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the child's nanny, KI's hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing, and two (2) additional traveling companions of KI's choosing (collectively referred to as KI's "entourage")' from the travel expenses clause in Section 5(c), the travel expenses covered are now uncertain and it's unclear on who else gets included with the agreement. This affects the scope of financial responsibility. Because the change occurs in this part of the sentance 'Travel expenses of KI in connection with all scheduled personal appearances under this Agreement, as well the travel expenses of KI's child shall be provided by Diplomat.' the responsibility of the company is only limited to those 2, but in the next sentence 'KI and the members of her entourage shall travel via first class air and portal-to-portal limousine ground transportation.' is saying that the the entourage travels first class, which contradicts the removal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Travel expenses of KI in connection with all scheduled personal appearances under this Agreement, as well the travel expenses of KI's child shall be provided by Diplomat. KI and the members of her entourage shall travel via first class air and portal-to-portal limousine ground transportation. In addition, KI and the members of her entourage shall be lodged in first class hotel accommodations and all of them shall be reimbursed for all meals and other incidental expenses in connection with such appearances. All the above travel expenses of KI and the members of her entourage shall be billed directly to Diplomat. For purposes of this paragraph (c), the term "travel expenses" shall include
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits 'the child's nanny, KI's hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing, and two (2) additional traveling companions of KI's choosing (collectively referred to as KI's "entourage")' from the list of whose travel expenses are covered by Diplomat. However, the subsequent sentences in the changed text still refer to 'KI and the members of her entourage' having first-class travel and accommodations, and reimbursement for expenses. This creates a contradiction because the entourage is no longer explicitly covered for travel expenses initially, yet is still mentioned in the context of travel, contradicting the explicit removal of their expenses from being covered. The contradicted text includes the same phrasing regarding the entourage and their travel expenses, thus highlighting the created contradiction in the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees that it will obtain, at its own expense, product liability insurance from a recognized insurance company which is qualified to do business in the State of California providing adequate protection (at least in the amount of $ ) for KI, Inc., KI and Diplomat against any claims, suits, loss or damage arising out of any alleged defects in the products.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees that it will obtain, at its own expense, product liability insurance from a recognized insurance company which is qualified to do business in the State of California providing adequate protection for KI, Inc., KI and Diplomat against any claims, suits, loss or damage arising out of any alleged defects in the products.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(at least in the amount of $ )' from the clause regarding product liability insurance, the exact amount of required coverage is no longer specified. Because of this, the insurance is ambiguous and unenforceable, therefore affecting the level of protection KI and KI, Inc receive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat hereby indemnifies KI, Inc. and KI, undertakes to defend KI, Inc. and KI against, and hold KI, Inc. and KI harmless from, any claims, suits, loss and damage (including attorneys' fees and costs) arising out of (a) any allegedly unauthorized use of any patent, process, idea, method, or device by Diplomat in connection with the products covered by this Agreement, (b) any alleged defects in the products covered by this Agreement, and (c) any other alleged action by Diplomat. Diplomat agrees that it will obtain, at its own expense, product liability insurance from a recognized insurance company which is qualified to do business in the State of California providing adequate protection for KI, Inc., KI and Diplomat against any claims, suits, loss or damage arising out of any alleged defects in the products. As proof of such insurance, a fully paid certificate of insurance naming KI, Inc. and KI as an insured party will be submitted to KI, Inc. by Diplomat for KI, Inc.'s prior approval before any product is distributed or sold, and at the latest within thirty (30) days after the date first written above. Any proposed change in certificates of insurance shall be submitted to KI, Inc. for its prior approval. KI, Inc. shall be entitled to a copy of the then prevailing certificate of insurance, which shall be furnished KI, Inc. by Diplomat. As used in the first 2 sentences of this paragraph 6, "KI, Inc." shall also include the officers, directors, agents, and employees of the KI, Inc., or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specified amount of product liability insurance, creating ambiguity regarding the level of protection required. This contradicts the original intent of the insurance clause within the contradicted text, which aims to provide 'adequate protection' for KI, Inc. and KI. Without a defined amount, the 'adequate protection' is not guaranteed and becomes unenforceable, thereby undermining the indemnification agreement that Diplomat has with KI, Inc. and KI.
